name: sustained_high_rate
description: Aggressive soak: 300 agents in sustained burst for 1 hour. Detects the case where input_rate barely exceeds worker_drain_rate (e.g. 95% saturation): the queue grows slowly but indefinitely and OOM appears after an hour, not after 2 minutes. Complements soak.json (which uses rate=0.5, well below the ceiling).
notes: Key difference vs soak.json: soak uses rate=0.5 (slow, detects per-session leaks), this uses rate=0 (continuous burst, detects chronic backpressure in m_workersQueue).
Reading the result: RSS should reach a plateau within the first 10 min. If it keeps rising monotonically for the full hour → the unlimited queue is retaining work.
Intended to run off-peak, not per-PR (1h per run).
If 300 agents saturate the sender on the test host, reduce to 200 and increase data_size to maintain the target message rate.
